description: Trigger and behavior cases for pipeline architecture: stage graph with blocking semantics, secret governance (OIDC, fork posture), artifact/cache rules, environment promotion, branch-protection alignment; manual-only because it edits pipeline files.

cases.prompt: Overhaul our GitHub Actions setup — PRs take 40 minutes, deploys use a stored AWS key, and half the checks are advisory. Design the pipeline and update the workflows.

assertions: Maps the current state first (what actually blocks merge, measured runtimes, secret usage per job) before proposing the stage graph.
Replaces the stored AWS key with OIDC federation to a scoped role, scopes secrets per job, and removes secret access from fork-PR-triggered runs.
Restructures for latency: build once, static checks first, test tiers per qa-automation-architect's blueprint (composed, not redesigned), moving slow suites off the merge path with named rationale.
Aligns branch-protection required checks to the merge-blocking stages exactly, pins action versions, and proves edited workflows with a lint/parse and a real run.

cases.prompt: Monorepo with six packages — every PR runs everything and people have started admin-merging to skip the queue. Fix the pipeline design.

assertions: Treats the admin-merging as the social failure signal it is: latency is handled as a control problem, with path-filter/affected-graph change detection so PRs run only impacted stages.
Keeps a merge-blocking floor (static checks + affected tests) and states what moved to post-merge/nightly and why.
Checks that bypass actors get closed back down once latency is fixed, per branch-protection alignment.

cases.prompt: Our integration tests can't run in parallel because they share fixtures — restructure the test project and fix the retry policy.

assertions: Test-suite structure, fixtures, and retry/flake policy are qa-automation-architect's blueprint; this skill only hosts the resulting tiers in the pipeline.

cases.prompt: The dependency scan keeps failing our releases — just set it to continue-on-error in the workflow so we can ship today.

assertions: Refuses to silently weaken a merge-blocking security check — routes through human-approval-boundary with the check, reason, and an expiry-dated skip if approved.
Proposes the real fix path (triage the scan findings per supply-chain-security-reviewer) alongside the gated exception.
Any approved skip is visibly reported in the pipeline, never a quiet green.
